key_insight: ESPO reduces wasted RL compute by detecting early reasoning failure during rollout and stopping trajectories before they continue generating low-value tokens.
engineering_relevance: For LLM RL training, early stopping can improve sample efficiency and sharpen credit assignment by turning failed rollouts into absorbing failure states with terminal penalties.
method: Monitors surrogate regret from already-computed sampling logits at each generation step.
Applies a smoothed cumulative threshold to detect likely failure on the fly.
Stops rollouts early and assigns a terminal reward so negative TD error concentrates near the failure step.
evidence_signal: On DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B, ESPO outperformed PPO on AIME 2024, AMC 2023, and MATH-500 while saving more than 20% of rollout tokens cumulatively.
watch_item: Early stopping depends on the quality of the failure detector and threshold calibration, so overly aggressive truncation could cut off valid long reasoning chains.
